top_categories.workout_context: 347 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Manali-Khardung La Cycling Championship's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 347 miles.
35,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Manali-Khardung La Cycling Championship's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 347 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At Manali-Khardung La Cycling Championship, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
35,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
101ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
347 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 35,000ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Manali-Khardung La Cycling Championship, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Manali-Khardung La Cycling Championship. Train it until it feels boring.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 347 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 347 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Manali-Khardung La Cycling Championship with 35,000ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Manali-Khardung La Cycling Championship demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mixed terrain at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 347 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and VO2max to prepare you for 347 miles of mixed terrain in Manali to Leh, Himachal Pradesh / Ladakh, India.
